Persistent Systems🔻8% mainly due to the market’s reaction to its Nagarro acquisition

Company has announced an all cash offer to acquire Nagarro SE at €81/share (acquired a significant premium)

Europe is the key strategic angle here. Persistent has been looking for a complementary acquisition in Europe, and Nagarro helps solve that gap. The combined company will have more than $600 Mn REV from Europe, along with more than $1.7 Bn from North America and over $400 Mn from the rest of the world.

Deal is also looks EPS accretive. On an illustrative pro forma TTM basis, the combined entity would have REV of $2,795 Mn, EBITDA of $463 Mn, EBIT of $337 Mn & PAT of $215 Mn.

The transaction assumes total debt of nearly $1.5 Bn, including acquisition funding & refinancing Nagarro’s existing debt.

Another concern is margins. Nagarro’s reported CY25 EBITDA margin was 12%. After adjustments, the margin improves to around 14-15%.

reports.text:
Persistent Systems - Acquire Nagarro European listed at $ 1.4 bln and bagged $650 mln deal over 6.5 years

Maintain Buy with 28% upside potential valuing at 34x on FY28E.for target of Rs. 6200.

Key Reads

It's a transformational moment for Persistent Systems. In one day, they announced:

A bold inorganic move that doubles their European footprint and diversifies away from US concentration

A massive organic deal worth USD 125m/year from an existing client.

We are positive because the earnings growth story doesn't break — in fact it accelerates — while no equity dilution occurs. The key risks (margin integration, ERP competitive intensity) are real but manageable if execution is disciplined. 

The stock is trading at a discount to its intrinsic value given this new earnings power, hence continuing to assign premium multiple of 34x.

**Stock SIP | Mutual fund SIP
****@Stocksip**

Oil & Gas Management Meetings (Delhi) – KTAs

Our analyst met the management teams of GAIL, Petronet LNG, and Oil India. Key highlights:

 GAIL 

• Gas transmission & marketing volumes have normalised post reopening of the Strait of Hormuz (June: 120 mmscmd vs 99 mmscmd in Mar'26).

• 1QFY27 expected to benefit from stronger marketing margins and improved petchem prices.

• Trades at 12.5x FY28E EPS; TP of ₹184 implies ~7% upside.

 Petronet LNG

• Dahej utilisation in 1QFY27 likely at 70–80% (vs 91% in 4QFY26), but volumes should normalise by end-July with a strong Q2.

• Key catalysts: Kochi–Mangalore–Bengaluru pipeline commissioning (4QFY27) and new Dahej capacity offtake tie-ups.

• Trades at 11x PE (LT avg: 14x); DCF-based TP implies ~28% upside.

 Oil India

• FY27 production growth likely to remain modest at 3–4%.

• Higher gas realisations from new wells delayed to end-FY28; Andaman discoveries remain years away from monetisation.

• We remain Neutral on Oil India and prefer ONGC, which offers similar production growth with better upside from higher new well gas realisations.

reports.text:
CLSA on Persistent
High conviction O-P TP Rs 6520
Announced acquisition of European listed IT services co, Nagarro, which strong digital engineering capabilities and FY26 annual revenue of US$1.14bn, or c.69% of Persistent’s FY26 revenue. At an enterprise value of US$1.4bn, it implies EV/sales of 1.2x, an EV/Ebitda of 9.6x & a PE multiple of 13x
Past two-year revenue Cagr for Nagarro was 5% with Ebit/Ebitda margins of 12.1%/15.6%, which are much lower than Persistent’s organic revenue growth of 18.1% (in US dollars) & its Ebitda margin of 18.4%
Acquisition is at a c.140% premium to Nagarro’s 25-June-2026 closing stock price.
Even at current purchase price, given massive derating in the sector globally, see c.6% EPS accretion
Hence, believe purchase is at an attractive price and should help Peristent achieve its FY31 vision of US$5bn revenue.

CITI on Persistent
Sell, TP Rs 4090
Announced intention to acquire Nagarro at EUR81 per share (140% premium to last disturbed closing price).
Nagarro is a German digital engineering company with ~18,500 employees
Acquisition will strengthen Persistent’s European presence; most key verticals would reach good scale ($300m+).
Implied EV/EBITDA of 10x CY26 Bloomberg consensus.
While acquisition does look expensive considering historic growth trajectory (& factoring in current valuations globally as well as other similar transactions in the sector), there is a strategic imperative (European presence) as well.
In near term, combined entity growth rate should be just about low double digit yoy
As per mgmt, the deal will be cash EPS accretive & EPS accretive (except one off costs) in year 1.
Persistent trades at ~33x FY27E consensus ests – significant premium to peers, and now investors will need to monitor integration risks as well near term

Nomura on Persistent
Neutral, TP Rs 5200
Persistent is acquiring a 21% stake in Nagarro, a Munich-headquartered digital engineering company delivering full-stack solutions across digital engineering, intelligent enterprise, and experience & design with a focus on industrial, consumer, TMT and BFSI verticals.
Nagarro recorded a total revenue of EUR1bn in CY25.
It has 18,500 employees spread across 40+ countries (13,500 in India, 3,000 in Europe, 500 in the US and 1,500 in RoW).
Approximately 35% of its revenue come from North America, 30% from Central Europe, 13% from rest of Europe & 23% from RoW.
Nagarro services 10 verticals of which auto, manufacturing and industrial forms 25%, retail 13.5% and financial services 12.4% of revenue
Co had adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.8% and EBIT margin of 8.3% in CY25 (1Q CY26, EBITDA margin: 15.6%, EBIT margin 12.1%).
Persistent intends to acquire a 100% stake in this company & delist it from German stock exchange through an open offer

Domestic PCB Manufacturing to Save ₹40K Cr Imports Annually: Vaishnaw

The Big Savings
India will save foreign exchange worth ~₹40,000 crore annually by slashing dependence on imported printed circuit boards (PCBs), now planned to be manufactured domestically

Liquid Outlook cooling solutions for data centres and AI infrastructure:

- The SFN liquid cooling skid assembly is a compact, plug-and-play unit that integrates stainless steel piping systems, precision-engineered metal hoses and metallic expansion joints into a closed-loop coolant distribution system designed to remove heat from high-density AI and compute hardware reliably, efficiently and without leakage.

- To accommodate the rapid scaling of demand, skid assembly manufacturing capacity was expanded from 2,000 units per annum in December 2025 to 6,000 units per annum as at 31 March 2026. Further additions in capacity to 9,000 units in Q2FY27 is on track, targeting total installed capacity of 15,000 units per annum by Q2FY27.
~ A.R. 26 - Aeroflex Ind.

Western battery makers try to keep bad cells below 1 per million. CATL, which makes roughly 4 in every 10 EV batteries sold worldwide, aims for 1 bad cell per billion, a thousand times stricter. 

They run AI across 6,800 quality checkpoints on their production lines, A single EV pack holds thousands of cells and one defective cell can start a fire. 

The gap between measuring quality per million versus per billion is not a numbers game, it is an entirely different manufacturing culture, and right now nobody in the West is close to matching it.

CATL chief technology officer for 8 years was Bob Galyen, who spent 21 years at General Motors. BMW 2012 contract forced CATL to meet German automotive quality standards when it was still a small company. 

This follows the same path Japan car industry took. Toyota learned quality systems from an American consultant, then used that discipline to outcompete Detroit. 

CATL did something similar, absorbed Western expertise and Western client standards, then scaled manufacturing precision to a level those same Western companies have not been able to match on their own

LG defective battery cells in the Chevy Bolt caused fires and forced a recall of every Bolt ever made, costing around $2 billion. 

Root cause was two manufacturing defects appearing in the same cell, exactly the kind of failure CATL billion level quality checks are designed to catch, CATL now holds 40% of the global EV battery market. 

When one bad batch can cost you $2 billion and your entire brand reputation, quality is not a department you fund. It is the thing that decides whether you survive in this industry at all.

Battery need everywhere :)

reports.text:
Value Chain of Capital Market Wealth Managers. 

1. Pure-Play Distribution Platforms (Monoproduct to Multi-product)
These companies primarily distribute third-party products (mutual funds, structured products) and earn trail commissions and placement fees. They own no manufacturing capability. Anand Rathi Wealth is the exemplar in this category explicitly and philosophically committed to pure distribution. The argument for this model: no conflict of interest, no capital consumption in manufacturing, pure operating leverage on AUM.

2. Integrated Wealth + Asset Management Platforms
These firms do both  distribute products and manufacture them. 360 ONE and Nuvama fall here. Manufacturing their own AIFs, PMS strategies, and credit funds allows them to earn higher fees (1.5-2.5% vs 0.7-1.0%), create unique product access that clients cannot get elsewhere, and keep more of the fee pool in-house rather than sharing it with third-party AMCs.

3. Asset Services / Market Infrastructure Players
Nuvama is the only listed pure-play that has a significant Asset Services (custody + clearing) business. This is market infrastructure not subject to the same competitive dynamics as wealth management, and with a recurring revenue structure that behaves like a toll road.

4. Technology-Enabled B2B2C Platforms
360 ONE's RESERVE program for HNI clients (₹5-50 crore) and its ET Money acquisition represent a new model: using technology to serve a lower-ticket segment at near-zero marginal cost per client. ET Money is a loss-making investment in building future distribution at scale.

Kotak Mahindra Bank CEO Ashok Vaswani to Step Down at End-2026

Kotak Mahindra Bank Managing Director & CEO Ashok Vaswani has decided not to seek reappointment after his three-year term ends on December 31, 2026, citing personal reasons

The bank has initiated the process to appoint a new MD & CEO and expects to complete the selection within the regulatory timeline

Vaswani took charge on January 1, 2024, succeeding founder Uday Kotak

During his tenure, he sought to accelerate the bank's growth through an inorganic expansion strategy, though outcomes were mixed

🏛️ Pricol

Legacy heavy engineering has historically suppressed valuations of newer, high-growth segments within Texmaco Rail. 

Rail infra and green energy are in secular demand driven by government capex cycles. Separation allows each entity to raise capital independently, aligned to its own growth trajectory.

reports.text:
Western battery makers try to keep bad cells below 1 per million. CATL, which makes roughly 4 in every 10 EV batteries sold worldwide, aims for 1 bad cell per billion, a thousand times stricter. 

They run AI across 6,800 quality checkpoints on their production lines, A single EV pack holds thousands of cells and one defective cell can start a fire. 

The gap between measuring quality per million versus per billion is not a numbers game, it is an entirely different manufacturing culture, and right now nobody in the West is close to matching it.

CATL chief technology officer for 8 years was Bob Galyen, who spent 21 years at General Motors. BMW 2012 contract forced CATL to meet German automotive quality standards when it was still a small company. 

This follows the same path Japan car industry took. Toyota learned quality systems from an American consultant, then used that discipline to outcompete Detroit. 

CATL did something similar, absorbed Western expertise and Western client standards, then scaled manufacturing precision to a level those same Western companies have not been able to match on their own

LG defective battery cells in the Chevy Bolt caused fires and forced a recall of every Bolt ever made, costing around $2 billion. 

Root cause was two manufacturing defects appearing in the same cell, exactly the kind of failure CATL billion level quality checks are designed to catch, CATL now holds 40% of the global EV battery market. 

When one bad batch can cost you $2 billion and your entire brand reputation, quality is not a department you fund. It is the thing that decides whether you survive in this industry at all.

Battery need everywhere :)

BIG UPDATE FOR HDFC BANK 

SENTIMENTAL POSITIVE 

*HDFC Bank update: No evidence found by external law firm*

- HDFC Bank announced the completion of an independent legal review conducted by external law firms following the concerns raised by former Independent Director Atanu Chakraborty in his resignation letter. After a three-month review involving thousands of documents, Board and committee minutes, and interviews with independent directors and senior management, the law firms concluded that Mr. Chakraborty’s allegations were not substantiated. 

- *Key findings:* No evidence was found in Board or committee records supporting his concerns. Mr. Chakraborty had opportunities to record any dissent or ethical concerns in meeting minutes but did not do so. 
- Interviews with directors and senior management did not corroborate his statements. 
- No contemporaneous evidence showed that he had raised objections regarding the Dubai matter or any other Board decisions.
- Despite repeated requests, Mr. Chakraborty did not participate in the legal review. 
- The external law firms found no factual basis to support Mr. Chakraborty’s statements, and the contemporaneous evidence was inconsistent with the allegations made in his resignation and subsequent public comments.

• VISL currently operates with 15mtpa iron ore capacity and 3mtpa steel capacity, supported by an integrated mining to steel value chain.

• Iron ore capacity is targeted to expand from ~15mtpa to 18.5mtpa by FY29, supported by beneficiation expansion and pellet capacity addition. Liberia assets is expected to contribute 10mtpa pellet feed concentrate by FY32.

• Integrated steel production planned to increase from ~3mtpa to 5mtpa by FY29, with a longer term expansion potential of upto 15mtpa.

• ESL plant Phase I expansion is nearing completion and is expected to double capacity from 1.6mtpa to 3.2mtpa by FY27, supported by existing infrastructure and land availability. 

• The company currently operates a 2mt beneficiation plant in Goa and plans to add 4mt of beneficiation capacity in Karnataka to improve ore quality and economics. Additionally, a new 2mt pellet plant is being set up in Odisha to strengthen raw material integration for Bokaro steel operations.

• Goa iron ore reserves carry ~57% Fe grade, with current production at 57–58% Fe, while Karnataka reserves are lower grade at 42-43% Fe, upgraded to ~51% Fe sales grade through beneficiation.

• Planned 3mt operations in Liberia are expected to generate ~USD 100mn in EBITDA by FY30, this would also help replace Karnataka mines’ EBITDA contribution of ~USD 80mn after lease expiry in 2032.

• Subject to receiving Stage 1 forest clearance, the key blast furnace (BF) expansion is largely ready and is expected to be commissioned within 3 months.

• Management targets ~4x EBITDA growth by FY29, driven by steel capacity expansion, and a higher contribution from value added products (VAP). The share of VAP is expected to increase from 59% in FY26 to ~100% by FY29E, supporting improved realizations and margin expansion. VAP mix would largely include wire rods, TMT, and high grade alloys

• The total planned capex is USD 1.2bn through FY29, of which USD 422mn has been approved and USD 268mn has already been deployed. Around 70% of the capex is expected to be funded through internal accruals, while the remaining 30% will be supported by a proforma net debt of ~Rs 25bn (as of May 2026), after demerger related adjustments. 

• planned capex is estimated at USD 130-150mn for FY27 which would  peak at ~USD 350mn each in FY28 and FY29.

• Capacity additions in DI pipes, wire rods, and TMT products are expected to strengthen and deepen the VISL’s value added steel portfolio. 

• Pig iron margins are expected to increase from USD 30-40/t to ~USD 60-65/t over time.

*Vedanta Ltd - Ferro Chrome*

• Vedanta plans to expand ferro chrome capacity from 145kt to 500kt by FY30, with the new expansion project expected to be commissioned in late FY27. The company targets EBITDA of ~USD 180mn by FY30 and aims to become one of India's largest ferro chrome producers.

• The business has strong raw material security through ownership of five mines (four chromite and one manganese) with reserves/resources of ~52 mt, providing visibility for over 25 years. Underground mining at Ostapal has commenced and is expected to improve ore quality and support future growth.

• Management expects realizations to increase from USD 1,100/t currently to USD 1,250/t by FY30 through higher-grade ore, improved product mix and a greater share of premium-sized products. Expansion into European and far east markets is also expected to support pricing improvement.

• Domestic market share is targeted to rise from 16% to 37%, supported by India's stainless steel demand growth of ~10% CAGR. The company is transitioning from trader-led sales to direct OEM relationships and long-term offtake agreements with major stainless steel producers.

reports.text:
• Production costs are expected to decline from USD 1,100/t in FY26 to USD 850/t in FY27 through operational efficiencies and mine optimization initiatives. Additionally, the planned 150 MW renewable power capacity will enable ~50% green ferrochrome production, enhancing access to CBAM-compliant export markets.

*Zinc International*

• Zinc International owns one of the world's largest undeveloped zinc resource portfolios, anchored by the Gamsberg asset in South Africa. The resource base provides a long mine life and supports management's long-term ambition of scaling production towards 1mt  of metal-in-concentrate (MIC).

• Growth will be driven through Gamsberg phase-II expansion, development of the Swartberg open-pit and underground mines, and the Namibia-based Gergarub project. These projects are expected to significantly increase production volumes and strengthen the asset portfolio over the next five years.

• MIC production is targeted to increase from ~340 kt in FY27 to ~500-550 kt by FY29 and ~800 kt by FY30. Underground mining initiatives, higher-grade ore access and debottlenecking projects are expected to improve both throughput and operational efficiency.

• Cost of production is expected to decline from USD 2,000/t currently to USD 1,200/t over the long term through economies of scale, improved mine planning and higher utilization levels. As a result, EBITDA is projected to increase from USD 160 mn in FY26 to nearly USD 1 bn by FY30. FY27 costs are targeted at USD 1,500/t

• Vedanta is evaluating a zinc smelter in South Africa, supported by its large resource base and potential preferential power arrangements.

*Copper*

• Management expects India's copper demand to reach ~3.2 mt by 2030, supported by growth in power, renewable energy, EVs and infrastructure. Demand in the GCC region, particularly Saudi Arabia, is also expected to grow strongly, creating significant opportunities for copper processing and manufacturing. 

• Vedanta aims to build a 1mt copper platform comprising 500 kt capacity in India and 500 kt in the middle east. The strategy encompasses refining, rod manufacturing, mining exploration and potential smelting projects to create an integrated copper value chain across both regions.

• The company is progressing with key growth projects, including the Saudi copper rod mill, which is expected to be commissioned by Oct-26 to cater to the region's expanding downstream copper ecosystem. Vedanta has also secured exploration licences in Saudi Arabia and is evaluating mining opportunities to strengthen raw material security and integration.

• Silvassa refinery production has increased to 222 kt and is targeted to reach ~230 kt in the coming months, utilizing the existing 250 kt capacity more efficiently. In parallel, management continues discussions with authorities regarding the potential restart of the Sterlite copper plant at Tuticorin under a proposed green copper framework.

• Copper volumes are expected to increase from 364 kt in FY27 to 713 kt by FY30, driven by contributions from Silvassa, Fujairah, the Saudi rod mill and future smelter projects. EBITDA is projected to grow from USD 90mn in FY27 to USD 297mn by FY30, supported by higher volumes, improved utilization and expansion-led growth.

Emerald Finance Limited Onboards Ebixcash Mobility Software India Limited, Delhi for Early Wage Access

Emerald Finance Limited has entered into a new employer partnership with Ebixcash Mobility Software India Ltd., Delhi, extending its Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program to their employees.

Through a salary-linked advance model, employees can access a portion of their earned wages at any point during the month - disbursed in coordination with the employer, helping address short-term liquidity needs.

By eliminating dependency on the fixed pay cycle, the program gives employees greater control over their finances. Repayments are managed through automatic payroll deductions at month end - keeping the process disciplined, transparent and stress-free.

This collaboration reflects Emerald Finance's continued commitment to expanding its EWA network across India, making earned wage access a standard employee benefit.

- Focus on primary supply misplaced

- Commodity outlook weakening

- Scrap replacing primary output

reports.text:
MACQUARIE ON INDIA CONSUMER

2019 monsoon season is instructive where while onset of monsoon was poor

In 2019, monsoon in Jun at 67%, while in July 105%, Aug 115%, Sep 152% of LPA

Resulting in no limited impact to agri production/ rural incomes

See rural focused plays like Britannia, Dabur at risk from potential hit to monsoons

Preferred plays in the consumer space include Titan, Lenskart, Marico and HUL

JEFFERIES ON REAL ESTATE

Industry wide double-digit sales growth in Apr-May

Easing geopolitical tensions has led to late quarter push for approvals by listed comp

Major launches by Oberoi, Prestige, GPL, Sobha, DLF are skewing late June/early July

Depending on how late qtr. launches get booked, we expect 20% + sales growth

See sub 10% for Lodha, Large decline for Prestige Estates and DLF YOY

JEFFERIES ON SYNGENE AND CONCORD

Recently interacted with Syngene and Concord Biotech management

Syngene identified gaps impacting RFP conversion & initiated corrective actions

FY27 will be a transition year for Syngene due to ongoing destocking impacts

Concord's FY26 weakness was driven by US destocking

Oncology, anti-infectives expected to be key near-term growth drivers for Concord

MACQUARIE ON INDIA CONSUMER

Rural sentiment at risk from weak monsoons

See limited risk to food inflation

Monsoon in latter months/ spatial distribution are key to track

Past periods suggest that weak monsoons can have a sharp impact on rural incomes

Negatives seems to be well priced in

Hardly any correlation of Hero Moto volume vs El Nino as against narrative

Positive volume growth, barring covid & regulatory disruptions

Risk-reward has turned highly favourable

**CLSA on SBI**

**Rating**
- Outperform
- Target Price: ₹1,275/share

**Loan Growth**
- Robust system-wide growth
- Driven by retail demand
- Corporate lending remains healthy
- Focus on profitable growth

**Management Commentary**
- Tactical approach to corporate lending
- Prioritizing profitability over volume
- Disciplined credit underwriting
- Balanced growth strategy

**Margin Outlook**
- FY27 NIM target: 3%
- Supported by repricing of T-Bill linked loans
- Confident of achieving guidance
- FCNR(B) impact on margins limited

**Liquidity**
- RBI's FCNR(B) measures
- Positive for system liquidity
- Improves funding environment
- Limited direct benefit to SBI margins

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality remains strong
- Stable despite geopolitical uncertainties
- Credit costs remain under control
- Healthy balance sheet

**Key Risk**
- El Niño remains the primary concern
- Weak monsoon could impact rural demand
- Potential slowdown in rural credit growth
- Agriculture-linked segments to be monitored

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA maintains an **Outperform** rating with a **₹1,275 target**, citing robust loan growth, confidence in achieving a **3% FY27 NIM**, strong asset quality, and disciplined lending. The key monitorable remains the monsoon, as a weak rainfall season could weigh on rural demand.

**Goldman Sachs on Bharat Dynamics**

**Rating**
- Sell
- Target Price: ₹1,275/share

**Order Outlook**
- HAL orders to be executed over 24-60 months
- Meaningful execution from FY28
- Better long-term revenue visibility
- Near-term execution remains limited

**Helina Missile Order**
- Ahead of expectations
- FY29 revenue estimates raised
- FY29 earnings estimates raised
- EPS increased by 1.4%

**Key Concerns**
- Near-term margin risks
- Higher external procurement mix
- Execution challenges remain
- Profitability could be impacted

**Investment View**
- Order inflows improving
- Revenue visibility strengthened
- Execution remains key monitorable
- Cautious on near-term earnings delivery

**Key Takeaway**
- Goldman Sachs maintains a **Sell** rating with a **₹1,275 target**. While the Helina order improves FY29 earnings estimates and order visibility, the brokerage remains concerned about execution timelines and potential margin pressure from a higher share of externally procured components.

Sri Priyanka Geo Commex IPO

The real story is not just revenue growth. It is working capital expansion.

The company’s working capital requirement is projected to rise from ₹17.27 crore in FY25 to ₹36.96 crore in FY27.

That is more than 2x in two years.

Using the FY23–FY25 average working capital turnover ratio of ~14.85x, this level of working capital can support revenue of nearly ₹549 crore in FY27.

For context, FY25 revenue was ₹266 crore.

So, the projected working capital base is implying almost 2x revenue potential.
